title: Nehanurpatti
tagline: Nehanurpatti — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Nehanurpatti is a much-loved spiritual town in Tamil Nadu, known for temples and pilgrimage.
overview.description: Neganur Patti is a 1 sq. kilometre village in Gingee taluk in Villuppuram district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The major occupation of the people living at this place is agriculture. In 2012 it had a population of 3000 people.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Sirukadambur
topPlaces.description: Sirukadambur is a village in the Sendurai taluk of Ariyalur district, Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Nangilikondan
topPlaces.description: Nangilickondan (also called Nangilickondan) is a village in Villupuram district, Tamil Nadu. It has located between Gingee and Tindivanam road.

topPlaces.name: Pennagar, Gingee
topPlaces.description: Penagar is an Indian Panchayat village located in Gingee taluk of Viluppuram district in the state of Tamil Nadu, India. According to the 2001 census, the village had a population of 2071, with a literacy rate of 59%.

topPlaces.name: Gingee Venkataramana Temple
topPlaces.description: The Gingee Venkataramana Temple, built in the 16th century is the largest temple in Gingee, in the Viluppuram district of Tamil Nadu. It was built by Muthialu Nayakan (Muthyala Nayaka) (1540 - 1550 CE) and dedicated to Venkateshwara.

topPlaces.name: Gingee Fort
topPlaces.description: Gingee Fort or Senji Fort (also known as Chenji, Chanchi, Jinji or Senchi) in Tamil Nadu, India is one of the surviving forts in Tamil Nadu, India. It is popularly known as Great Wall of South India.

topPlaces.name: Mel Sithamur Jain Math
topPlaces.description: Jina Kanchi Jain Math, Melsithamur, is a Jain Matha that is located near Gingee, Villupuram district, Tamil Nadu, India. It is the primary religious center of the Tamil Jain community.

topPlaces.name: Vallam block
topPlaces.description: The Vallam block is a revenue block in the Viluppuram district of Tamil Nadu, India. It has a total of 66 panchayat villages.

topPlaces.name: Viranamur
topPlaces.description: Viranamur is a village in the southern state Tamil Nadu in India. It is located in Gingee Taluk, Villupuram District of Tamil Nadu.

items.note: Sirukadambur is a village in the Sendurai taluk of Ariyalur district, Tamil Nadu, India.
Nangilickondan (also called Nangilickondan) is a village in Villupuram district, Tamil Nadu. It has located between Gingee and Tindivanam road.

Penagar is an Indian Panchayat village located in Gingee taluk of Viluppuram district in the state of Tamil Nadu, India. According to the 2001 census, the village had a population of 2071, with a literacy rate of 59%.

The Gingee Venkataramana Temple, built in the 16th century is the largest temple in Gingee, in the Viluppuram district of Tamil Nadu. It was built by Muthialu Nayakan (Muthyala Nayaka) (1540 - 1550 CE) and dedicated to Venkateshwara.
Gingee Fort or Senji Fort (also known as Chenji, Chanchi, Jinji or Senchi) in Tamil Nadu, India is one of the surviving forts in Tamil Nadu, India. It is popularly known as Great Wall of South India.
Jina Kanchi Jain Math, Melsithamur, is a Jain Matha that is located near Gingee, Villupuram district, Tamil Nadu, India. It is the primary religious center of the Tamil Jain community.
The Vallam block is a revenue block in the Viluppuram district of Tamil Nadu, India. It has a total of 66 panchayat villages.
Viranamur is a village in the southern state Tamil Nadu in India. It is located in Gingee Taluk, Villupuram District of Tamil Nadu.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Nehanurpatti?
faq.a: The best time to visit Nehanurpatti is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

The nearest airport is Chennai International Airport (111 km) and the nearest railway station is Chennai Central (126 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Nehanurpatti?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Nehanurpatti at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Nehanurpatti?
faq.a: Stays in Nehanurpatti start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Nehanurpatti?
faq.a: The top attractions are Sirukadambur, Nangilikondan, Pennagar, Gingee, Gingee Venkataramana Temple, Gingee Fort.
